location.office_time_required: four days a week in the office for those near one of our offices; fully remote program available

level_signals: 6+ years of experience driving improvements in complex, cross-functional environments

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience driving improvements in complex, cross-functional environments — ideally in customer experience, program management, operations, or product enablement roles.
You've managed initiatives that improved customer satisfaction, automation, or efficiency in service or support ecosystems.
Member-Obsessed Mindset: You act as the voice of the member in every decision, ensuring that improvements elevate satisfaction and trust.
Strong Ownership Mentality: You independently drive programs from insight to execution, balancing urgency with rigor and transparency.
Cross-Functional Influence: You navigate complex partnerships across Member Experience, Product, and Risk, building trust through credibility, clarity, and results.
Strategic Problem-Solving Skills: You break down complex challenges, use structured frameworks, and anticipate downstream implications to deliver sustainable solutions.

Data-Driven Decision Making: You can interpret trends from multiple data sources and use them to guide prioritization and assess impact—you have experience with tools like Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or other data exploration tools and feel comfortable navigating them.
Adaptability and Resilience: You stay grounded and focused through ambiguity, leading others through change with composure and clarity.
~10–15% travel is the commitment for this role

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Managing end-to-end support or customer journeys in a high-growth environment
Partnering with Product, Engineering, or Analytics to design and deliver support tooling or automation
Using data and insights (e.g., NPS, contact drivers, AHT) to shape priorities and measure impact
Leading process optimization, knowledge management, or quality initiatives within customer experience organizations
Working with outsourced or vendor operations teams to drive consistent experience and performance

scope.surface: Member Experience (MX) team — Spending team; focus on payments, cards, transfers, and transaction-related support experience
core_responsibilities: Manage entry points, routing, automation, knowledge management, and resolution quality for new and core products
Partner with Product, Engineering, Analytics, and other teams to deliver new features optimized for member experience
Use analytics to prioritize high-impact initiatives, track outcomes, and inform decision-making
Test support flows (e.g., A/B, holdouts), evaluate outcomes, and scale what works
Drive accountability for member contact rates for specific products and features
Drive standard operating procedures and workflows that are compliant and up-to-date
Translate contact taxonomy, NPS/voice of customer insights, QA data, and sentiment data into measurable impact plans

green_flags: Fully remote program available, not just hybrid
Equity and bonus mentioned in addition to base salary
Generous parental leave (up to 24 weeks for birthing parents)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Spending team — payments, cards, transfers)
Specific metrics callouts (contact rate, NPS, AHT) signal data-maturity of team
red_flags: 10–15% travel required — may be overlooked given remote-first framing
In-office policy is described for those near an office (four days/week) but role is also tagged remote — ambiguity about whether remote candidates ever need office time
Visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
Nice-to-have experience areas are listed as a bulleted group under a single sentence, making severity classification ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as fintech/neobank)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as KPIs)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from #LI-Remote tag and 'fully remote program' mention)
ambiguities: In-office policy section describes four days/week in-office for those near an office, but the role is tagged #LI-Remote and mentions a 'fully remote program.' It is unclear whether remote employees are ever required to come into an office.

Nice-to-have items are introduced with 'Experience in one or more of the following areas is especially valuable' — the phrase 'especially valuable' is softer than a hard requirement but not explicitly labeled 'preferred' or 'bonus.' Classified as nice_to_haves (soft) per JD language.
location_pay_differential: JD states 'The actual base salary offered may be higher, depending on your location' implying geographic differentiation, but no explicit tiers are given.
